signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Shock + Carry + Options Skew
signals.text: USD/JPY at 162.46 — Trump's 20% Hormuz shipping fee and Iran blockade sent WTI oil surging 9.48%, driving a bear flattener regime in US yields (10Y at 4.609%). Despite the geopolitical shock, the +275bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage sustains the bid as VIX remains at 17.2 (normal volatility). However, institutional hedging is accelerating: 25d RR at -1.58 signals the strongest put skew in the G10, diverging from the crowded JPY COT LF net short of -104,231. Carry dominates the spot trend, but options markets are pricing aggressive protection against a BoJ-led or intervention-driven reversal.

signals.title: USD/CHF — G10 Bilateral Theme + Vol Risk Premium
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.8147 — G10 scorecard identifies a significant bilateral divergence as USD outperforms (+0.369%) while CHF lags (-0.726%). The 1.095% spread is driven by the massive +375bp Fed-SNB carry advantage and a 'Strong Positive' USD CESI (+63). Volatility markets are pricing extreme tail-risk: USD/CHF IV at 24.8% vs HV30 of 6.3% creates a +18.4% Vol Risk Premium, indicating the market is paying a massive premium for protection against a CHF short-squeeze despite the carry-driven uptrend.
signals.watch_level: Watch for Fed's Waller comments or US CPI to validate the restrictive real rate of +1.47%.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4156 — The historical inverse correlation between CAD and Oil has suffered a major break (corr -0.898 vs norm +0.006, z=-2.02σ); CAD is failing to capture the 9.48% WTI surge due to the +150bp USD carry advantage and BoC hold bias. Retail sentiment is 79% short, fading the move into the BoC Monetary Policy Report. With COT LF net short at -87,846 (22.6% long), the pair remains a 'buy the dip' candidate as long as the US-CA real rate differential favors the USD.
signals.watch_level: BoC Press Conference at 10:45 (Governor Macklem) for any shift in hold bias.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Technicals
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5750 — Price is holding below the 200-day moving average as bears target 0.5700. RBNZ 2.5%. If the NZ QSBO data (Tuesday Asia) surprises to the upside, the crowded spec short position faces significant unwind risk. NZD 1W avg performance (+0.160%) suggests the currency is showing relative resilience despite the DXY strength.
signals.watch_level: NZ QSBO release Tuesday Asia session.
